sections.heading: What is a Service?
sections.content: A Service runs background tasks without a UI. Types: started (runs until stopped), bound (runs while clients are connected), foreground (persistent notification, higher priority). Services run on the main thread by default — offload work to background threads.

sections.heading: When to Use
items: Coroutines in ViewModel: short-lived background work
Avoid plain started Service for network/db tasks
Android 14+: declare foreground type in manifest

sections.heading: Service Lifecycle
sections.content: onCreate() → onStartCommand()/onBind() → work → onDestroy(). Clean up threads, receivers, and resources in onDestroy().

sections.heading: Foreground Service Steps
items: Use WorkManager or coroutines if user visibility not required

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Use foreground services for persistent, user-visible tasks. Always startForeground(), declare types in manifest, run work on background threads via coroutines, and stopSelf() when done. Prefer WorkManager for deferrable tasks, and coroutines in ViewModel for short-lived background work without a Service.
